Barjeel Digital Markets DIFC Ltd Privacy, DSAR, and Retention Note for UAE PDPL compliance (fictional).
We process PDPL personal data such as Emirates ID token references, trade licence copy metadata, family-origin details for KYC appropriateness, and role-based contact mailboxes.
For educational use, our Crypto Academy webinars and ESG climate dashboards use synthetic or anonymised datasets and carry no issuer-sensitive content.
Cyber incident tabletop exercises use fake tickets and invalid identifiers (e.g., EID ****-****-1234, Licence AB-12345X) to avoid real PII.
Insider-list training portals display sample names only; operational insider lists for real deals are maintained in a segregated system and are not populated during demos.
Prior to a public filing, the issuer Sand Reef Protocol ADGM Limited’s draft ADGM token offer timetable and unresolved breach report are treated as non-public market-sensitive information and recorded on the restricted insider list.
Information barriers are enforced through separate network shares, monitored role-based mailboxes (e.g., syndicate@barjeeldigital.example), and blackout reminders that are purely procedural notifications.
Commercial term examples in procurement playbooks (e.g., vendor rate cards for cloud storage) are operational and not linked to any issuer or token listing.
Collection purposes include onboarding, DIFC/ADGM regulatory reporting, and satisfaction of SCA and DFSA/FSRA recordkeeping; marketing consents cover newsletters about crypto products and climate reporting features.
DSAR intake is via privacy@barjeeldigital.example or toll-free 800-0000; valid requests must include generic form labels only and will never require a full Emirates ID number.
We store KYC files for 6 years after relationship end; insider-list records tied to actual market events are retained for 5 years per local market conduct guidance; training materials are refreshed quarterly and immediately purge placeholders.
International transfers are limited to processors in ADGM, UAE mainland, and the EEA; when outside an adequate jurisdiction, we use PDPL-compliant contractual clauses and transfer risk assessments.
We do not sell personal data, and any crypto product names such as Desert Falcon Coin or Oasis Chain Validator are marketing examples, not live issuers.
If we run a public bug bounty about cyber resilience, submissions are anonymised and stored separately from operational incident logs.
Unannounced ESG-linked commercial terms under discussion with Palm Crescent Renewables PJSC for a potential ADGM green token framework must remain within the information barrier and are not to be shared beyond the deal team.
You may opt out of marketing at any time, and opting out does not affect your ability to access basic account services.
